relevant_cases.verbatim_excerpt: A 14-year-old girl with a known diagnosis of Crouzon syndrome presented for correction of midface and orbital hypoplasia. She had no cardiovascular, gastr
relevant_cases.relevance_notes: MASSIVE intraoperative hemorrhage (1L) during LeFort III surgery. Post-procedural hypotension (50/30 mmHg), tachycardia (120-170 bpm), hemodynamic collapse requiring fluids and blood transfusion.

relevant_cases.relevance_notes: Postoperative pseudoaneurysm from orthopedic hardware (dynamic hip screw). Delayed presentation with thigh mass after procedure. Represents post-procedural vascular complication.

relevant_cases.relevance_notes: RUPTURED renal artery aneurysm causing significant hemoglobin drop (10.5 → 6.5 g/dL). Acute hemorrhagic presentation with hemodynamic compromise requiring blood transfusion.
